headline: LOW Q1 Deep Dive: Pro Segment, Online Growth, and AI Initiatives Drive Stable Performance
summary: Home improvement retailer Lowe’s (NYSE:LOW) reported Q1 CY2026 results beating Wall Street’s revenue expectations, with sales up 10.3% year on year to $23.08 billion. The company expects the full year’s revenue to be around $93 billion, close to analysts’ estimates. Its non-GAAP profit of $3.03 per share was 2.1% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Trump said he'd 'remember' companies that didn't apply for tariff refunds. Many of them are anyway

headline: Jharonne Martis: The divide in consumer income cohorts is very evident
summary: Jharonne Martis, Director of Consumer Research at LSEG, says higher fuel prices and inflation are pressuring lower income consumers while discount retailers and value focused companies continue gaining traction.

headline: Alan McKnight: This is not 1999 but some names are getting frothy
summary: Alan McKnight, Chief Investment Officer at Regions Wealth Management, says strong corporate earnings and resilient consumer spending are supporting markets despite inflation, geopolitical tensions and IPO froth.
